name: Caramel Fried Apples or Bananas
recipeInstructions:
1. In a bowl, mix together the flour, cornstarch, and baking powder.
2. Add water and stir until smooth.
3. Peel and core the apples; cut each apple into 3 wedges. If using bananas, peel and cut into 1/2 inch thick diagonal slices.
4. Place the fruit in the batter and turn to coat evenly.
5. Pour oil into a deep pan to a depth of about 1 1/2 inches. Heat oil to 350 degrees.
6. Using chopsticks or a spoon, lift one piece of fruit at a time from the batter and let excess batter drip off. Lower the fruit into hot oil.
7. Cook several pieces at a time until the coating is golden brown (about 2 minutes). Remove with a slotted spoon and drain on paper towels.
8. Generously oil a flat serving dish. Fill a bowl to the brim with ice cubes; cover with water.
9. In a ten-inch frying pan, combine sugar, warm water, and salad oil. Stir to blend.
10. Place the pan over high heat. When the mixture begins to bubble (about one minute), shake the pan continuously to prevent burning.
11. Continue cooking and shaking the pan until the syrup just turns a pale straw color (about 9 minutes).
12. Immediately remove from heat, add sesame seeds, and swirl to mix. The syrup will continue to cook after you remove it from heat; the color will turn golden in a few seconds.
13. Drop the fruit into the syrup and swirl to coat evenly.
14. Using two spoons, immediately remove each piece of fruit and place on the oiled dish so the pieces do not touch.
15. At the table, dip each piece of fruit in ice water so the coating hardens and the fruit cools. Serve.
